summary: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck south-central Turkey at 4:17am local time near Kahramanmaras.
Focus was roughly 10km deep, about 24km south of Kahramanmaras.
Eleven minutes after the main shock, a magnitude 6.7 aftershock hit the same area.
A second major quake of magnitude 7.7 struck at 1:24pm near Elbistan, about 30 miles northwest of Kahramanmaras.
Around 8,000 people had been rescued from collapsed buildings across Turkey by this date.
First aid convoys finally reached northwest Syria after Western governments and the UN arranged logistics.
Rescuers in Turkey continued pulling survivors from rubble more than a week after the quakes.
Most rescue operations had wound down except in the heavily damaged provinces of Hatay and Kahramanmaras.
A magnitude 6.4 aftershock struck Hatay province, killing three and injuring more than 200.
Damage inspections counted around 164,000 buildings as destroyed or severely damaged across the affected zone.
Combined death toll crossed 50,000 across Turkey and Syria.
A magnitude 5.6 aftershock brought down more damaged buildings in southern Turkey, killing at least one.
International pledges to Turkey and Syria for relief and reconstruction topped roughly 7.5 billion dollars.
President Erdogan pledged to deliver 319,000 new homes within a year for displaced survivors.
Turkish authorities announced arrests of construction contractors linked to the building collapses.
By this point AFAD had logged over 30,000 aftershocks across the affected region.
World Bank and UN Development Programme estimated reconstruction cost between 35 and 100 billion dollars.
A 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck southeastern Turkey near Gaziantep, shaking across Syria, Lebanon and Cyprus.
AFAD named Kahramanmaras, Gaziantep, Sanliurfa, Diyarbakir, Adana, Adiyaman, Malatya, Osmaniye, Hatay and Kilis as worst-hit provinces.
Turkey declared a three-month state of emergency in the ten earthquake-stricken provinces.
Hundreds of aftershocks were reported overnight; officials warned residents against re-entering damaged structures.
Combined death toll exceeded 11,000; rescuers battled freezing weather and blocked roads.
First UN cross-border aid entered rebel-held northwest Syria via the Bab al-Hawa crossing.
Survivor rescues continued past the critical 72-hour window as death toll climbed above 23,000.
Combined death toll passed 29,000; Syrian government approved new cross-border humanitarian routes.
A teenage girl was pulled alive from rubble roughly 10 days after the quake.
The UN launched a one billion dollar flash appeal for earthquake victims in Turkey.
UN also launched a 397 million dollar appeal for Syria response needs.
Two men and a teenage boy were rescued in Hatay almost 12 days after the quake.
A new 6.3 magnitude tremor in the Turkey-Syria border area killed three and injured hundreds.
Aftershocks on this day brought additional building collapses and further casualties in Antakya.
Death toll climbed above 50,000, with 44,218 in Turkey and 5,914 in Syria.
Turkish prosecutors pursued charges against builders and inspectors over collapsed structures.
